ElementDescription: The NDAR Global Unique Identifier (GUID) for research subject
Subject ID how it's defined in lab/project
Date on which the interview/genetic test/sampling/imaging/biospecimen was completed. MM/DD/YYYY
Age in months at the time of the interview/test/sampling/imaging.
Notes: Age is rounded to chronological month. If the research participant is 15-days-old at time of interview, the appropriate value would be 0 months. If the participant is 16-days-old, the value would be 1 month.
M = Male; F = Female; O=Other; NR = Not reported

ElementDescription: Description of Relationship meaning, goals, and current status
Notes: Qualitative interview text data. Themes covered: A) romantic relationship meaning, B) satisfaction linked to relationship, C) importance of relationship, D) relationship goals, E) Settling down, F) Current relationship

ElementDescription: Description of sexual socialization, influence of relationship and sexual control
Notes: Qualitative interview text data. Themes covered: A) Upbringing affected sexual identity, B) sexual identity shaped by relationship, C) take control of sexual life

ElementDescription: Description of stress control, kids, and decision making
Notes: Qualitative interview text data. Themes covered: A) Coping with stressors, B) Have kids or plan for kids, C) Decision making autonomy

ElementDescription: Description of contraceptive, protection, STI/HIV status communication
Notes: Qualitative interview text data. Themes covered: A) Contraceptive use, B) sexual health protection, C) knowing partner's STI/HIV status

ElementDescription: Description of STI/HIV information source and knowledge
Notes: Qualitative interview text data. Themes covered: A) STI/HIV information source, B) Knowledge on STI/HIV prevention, C) Knowledge on STI/HIV treatment
Qualitative interview text data. Themes covered: A) Anticipated HIV stigma

ElementDescription: Description of HIV Pre-Exposure Prophylaxis (PrEP) comfort, access, availability, and acceptability
Notes: Qualitative interview text data. Themes covered: A) Level of comfort for using PrEP, B) PrEP access and availability, C) PrEP acceptability

ElementDescription: Description of technology accessibility, online healthcare information seeking, and online resources
Notes: Qualitative interview text data. Themes covered: A) How access internet and technology, B) Devices, C) Online healthcare information seeking, D) Online resources sought

ElementDescription: Description of interest in telehealth, content of telehealth sessions, service acceptability and accessibility
Notes: Qualitative interview text data. Themes covered: A) Interest in telehealth, B) content of telehealth sessions, C) service acceptability and accessibility
